claimText: Black holes do not emit or reflect light and are detected indirectly through their gravitational effects on surrounding stars and gas.
evidenceExcerpt: Black holes do not emit or reflect light; they are detected by their gravitational effects on surrounding stars and gas.
examUse: Statement-based question on black hole properties and detection methods.
distractorHooks: Black holes are detected by the intense light they emit
Black holes reflect cosmic microwave background radiation

claimText: Low- and medium-mass stars, such as the Sun, evolve into White Dwarfs at the end of their life cycle.
evidenceExcerpt: Low- and medium-mass stars (like the Sun) evolve into White Dwarfs.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching star mass to its final evolutionary stage.
distractorHooks: Low-mass stars evolve into neutron stars
The Sun will eventually become a black hole

claimText: High-mass stars evolve into Neutron Stars following a supernova explosion.
evidenceExcerpt: High-mass stars evolve into Neutron Stars after a supernova explosion.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching star mass to its final evolutionary stage.
distractorHooks: High-mass stars evolve into white dwarfs
High-mass stars bypass the supernova stage to become neutron stars

claimText: Very high-mass stars collapse into Black Holes after undergoing a supernova explosion.
evidenceExcerpt: Very high-mass stars evolve into Black Holes after a supernova explosion.
examUse: Statement-based question on the formation of black holes.
distractorHooks: Very high-mass stars evolve into white dwarfs
Black holes are formed exclusively from the collision of neutron stars

claimText: Black holes are classified into types including Stellar-Mass, Intermediate-Mass, and Supermassive Black Holes.
evidenceExcerpt: Types of black holes include Stellar-Mass, Intermediate-Mass (IMBH), and Supermassive Black Holes.
examUse: Statement-based question on black hole classification.
distractorHooks: Black holes only exist as supermassive entities at the center of galaxies
There are only two types of black holes: stellar and supermassive
